title: Regime-specific experts for dry, wet/intermediate, and testing-like domains
summary: Published pit-stop work explicitly notes dry-race pit calls are largely degradation/track-position driven, while wet/intermediate stops are much more reactive.
rationale: Published pit-stop work explicitly notes dry-race pit calls are largely degradation/track-position driven, while wet/intermediate stops are much more reactive. Pre-season testing is also not representative of normal race intent. A single global model likely averages incompatible policies.
implementation_hint: Train separate experts for at least: dry compounds (`SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD`), wet/intermediate compounds, and `Pre-Season Testing` rows if present. Optionally add a Monaco/special-rules expert. Blend by a simple gate from current row metadata, and keep the main dry model free from wet/testing noise.
expected_effect: Lower bias from mixing fundamentally different decision processes; likely most useful on rare but strategically distinct regimes.
risk: Sample sizes shrink fast, so some experts may become unstable; only split where counts are adequate and compare against a shared fallback model.
